rustyscabbard.name: The Rusty Scabbard
rustyscabbard.message:
Hi there — I hope your scabbards are rusty and your patrons are thirsty.

Would you give MeepleUp a try with your board game crowd? It's a free app I built for groups that game together consistently — players RSVP, nominate games, vote on what to play, and show up already knowing what's on the table. Less guessing, more gaming.

Full disclosure: MeepleUp is an early release, and it's been 1,000+ hours of solo work. If something feels off, I'd genuinely rather hear it directly at contact@meepleup.com than see it in a review.

If it lands well with your regulars, passing it along to another owner you trust would mean more to me than any review. And if you feel like leaving a rating on the App Store or Google Play, that helps too.

meepleup.com or search "MeepleUp" on the App Store / Google Play.

Thanks,
Rhys Smoker

legendarygames-lex.name: Legendary Games
legendarygames-lex.message:
Hi there — I hope your days are staying legendary.

Would you give MeepleUp a try with your regulars? It's a free app I built for groups that game together consistently — players RSVP, nominate games, vote on what to play, and arrive knowing what's hitting the table. With Kentucky's largest play space, you've got the room — MeepleUp helps your crowd fill it with the right game.

I'll be upfront — this is an early release and a solo build, probably 1,000+ hours in. If you hit anything rough around the edges, please reach out at contact@meepleup.com before leaving a review.

If your crowd takes to it, a word to another owner you respect would honestly mean more to me than a five-star review. A rating on the App Store or Google Play is always appreciated too.

meepleup.com or search "MeepleUp" on the App Store / Google Play.

Thanks,
Rhys Smoker

Would you give it a try with your crowd? It's a free app I built for groups that game together consistently — players RSVP, nominate from a shared library, vote on what to play, and show up already knowing what's hitting the table. Coffee, baked goods, and a plan — the ideal game day.

One honest note: it's an early release, built solo over 1,000+ hours. If anything doesn't land right, I'd much rather hear from you directly at contact@meepleup.com than lose you to a one-star review.

If it clicks with your group, sharing it with another owner in your network would go a long way — further than any rating. Though if you want to leave one on the App Store or Google Play, I won't say no.

meepleup.com or search "MeepleUp" on the App Store / Google Play.

Thanks,
Rhys Smoker

punkouter-gnv.name: PunkOuter Games
punkouter-gnv.message:
Hi there — I hope the punk is outer and the games are inner.

Would you give MeepleUp a try with your regulars? It's a free app I built for groups that game together consistently — players RSVP, nominate games, vote on what to play, and arrive knowing what's on the table. With private rooms, open tables, and a craft beer bar, your space is set up for real game nights — MeepleUp helps your regulars plan them.

Worth saying: it's an early release and I built it alone — somewhere north of 1,000 hours. If it's not quite right yet, contact@meepleup.com beats a bad review every time.

If it earns a place in your regulars' routine, telling another owner about it would mean the world — more than any review. A rating on the App Store or Google Play is a nice bonus if you feel so inclined.

meepleup.com or search "MeepleUp" on the App Store / Google Play.

Thanks,
Rhys Smoker

lochcafe-dul.name: The Loch Café & Games
lochcafe-dul.message:
Hi there — I hope the loch is unlocked and the coffee is on.

Would you give it a try with your regulars? It's a free app I built for groups that game together consistently — players RSVP, nominate from a shared library, vote on what to play, and arrive knowing what's on the table. Duluth's first cafe-gameshop and MeepleUp seem made for each other.

The app can also sync with BoardGameGeek or scan a collection by photo, which is handy for venues with an in-house game library.

Honest disclosure: solo project, early release, 1,000+ hours. If something needs fixing, contact@meepleup.com is the right place — not a review.

If your people enjoy it, the best thing you could do is mention it to another owner you know. That kind of peer word-of-mouth is how this grows. A rating on the App Store or Google Play is welcome too.

meepleup.com or search "MeepleUp" on the App Store / Google Play.

Thanks,
Rhys Smoker

Would you give it a try with your crowd? It's a free app I built for groups that game together consistently — players RSVP, nominate from a shared library, vote on what to play, and show up already knowing what's hitting the table. Tim and Karissa have built something special in Appleton — MeepleUp is a free tool to help your regulars make the most of every visit.

The app can also sync with BoardGameGeek or scan a collection by photo, which is handy for venues with an in-house game library.

One more thing: early release, built solo, 1,000+ hours in. If anything feels unfinished, contact@meepleup.com is where I want to hear it — before it becomes a review.

If it works for your crowd, a recommendation to another owner you respect would mean more to me than a hundred reviews. And if you do want to leave a rating on the App Store or Google Play, I'd be grateful.
